entry.title: How the Schedule line in MD04 resultant table filtered from the EKET table?
entry.content: For my master thesis i want to recreate md04 transaction result in power bi. found out it is from EKET and EKES tables but not all the schedule lines in EKET table are shown as the result of the MD04 table. how the filtration works. what is the criteria that the schedule lines in EKET table to be shown in md04 result of that particular material?

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite Monthly Webinar - August 2024
entry.content:
Hello Jodie, 

The webinar is open for both internal and external participants

entry.title: Re: Is SAP and Sustainability a Myth ?
entry.content:
I probably shouldn't say this, but my company is working really hard to make the future sustainable, with a focus on a circular economy. Imagine cutting physical and energy costs by using technology, workflow and automation (SAP Build Process Automation 😏)—resulting in fewer emissions and a better environment.

Another, small but significant, change is that SAP has been pushing for years to use HANA DB and CDS views. We're trying to move all the logic to the lower layers of the architecture, and you know what? By doing this, we reduce emissions because the processor handling the calculations has less work, which means it's consuming less energy. When most of the computation is done in the database, it should result in lower CO2 emissions.

I'd love for @qmacro  to correct me if needed, I know he cares about this topic. 😁

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite Monthly Webinar - August 2024
entry.content:
Hi, 

On the right side of this page, you can find a calendar blocker which you can use to join the meeting

entry.title: Re: Reminder email of open tasks in My Inbox
entry.content:
Hi,

You can configure the system so that approvers receive email notifications when a work item is created. By default, approvers are also informed by means of notifications in the SAP Fiori launchpad. Copy the pre delivered email template SWF_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS into the custom name space. The custom template must follow the following pattern:
  • <YY1>_<Scenario ID>_CRT_ALL> or
  • <YY1_<Scenario ID>_CRT_<Node_ID>
For more information on other cases where email notifications are possible, see section Maintain Email Templates in the How to Configure topic. (For example, you can also use the Overdue Deadline Email Notification option to notify approvers about created tasks by setting small deadline overdue times (for example, 1 minute): The corresponding email template used in overdue deadline notifications allow you to access and provide business object data in the email body.)

For example:

For more information about the configuration of the Maintain Email Templates app, see How to Configure the Flexible Workflow for Purchase Requisitions. In the interactive graphic, choose the Optional: Maintain Email Templates section.

Workflow features for Purchase Requisitions

Best regards,

Krithika

entry.title: Re: How to generate a Credit Note in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Pu...
entry.content:
Hello Adrian, The invoice correction process in S/4HANA Cloud is handled in the scope item BKL. But this correction is pertaining to amount differences, primarily. 

In your scenario, where you need correct the material description, you may not have to via credit note. Material description firstly needs to be updated in the Product/Material Master. This will take care of correction in the invoice. In case it has been already uploaded in the SUNAT, then only option I can think of is cancel the invoice and redo the invoicing process with correct material description.

Thank You.

entry.title: Re: MDK: Problem accessing the value of a field
entry.content: I was capturing the source of the information wrong. Your syntax was helpful in simplifying, thank you very much.

Vijayendra

Thank you,

Krithika

entry.title: Australia Payroll - Implementation Suggestion
entry.content:
SAP-PY-AU

Dear Experts,

We have Just finished implementing Indian Payroll and have started for Australia Payroll MOL 13.

I do have a few Queries for Shema Q000.

1. If you have any implementation document then sharing the same would help us in implementing Australia Payroll.

2. Configuration Pertaining to the carry forward and brought forward wage type and pcr and schema where it needs to be placed.

3. For Hourly Payment, where the employee's basic pay has got a Basic Pay wage type where we would be placing the 7dollars as the per hour rate. what would be the entire procedure to calculate the entire salary for the employee for the month.

4. Superannuation and Income tax Australia.

5. Any Standard Table to be maintained while implementing Australia Payroll.

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: Re: My geekiest hobby is tracking tagged ocean sharks—it’s the perfect way to clear my mind and refo
entry.content: TJ (A dolphin) who swims around San diego, LA.

entry.title: Re: Auto Approval of flexible workflow based on Custom Co...
entry.content:
Hi,

The BADI Automatic Release of Purchase order is to be set active in Manage workflows for Purchase orders app.

If there are no active workflows configured other than automatic release, the PO will automatically be approved without referencing to any pre-conditions.

SAP has delivered a set of pre-conditions and step conditions. Customers can additionally define their own pre-condition / step condition using the custom BADI. New conditions can be defined using custom BADI SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF.

Please refer to https://me.sap.com/notes/2841783

Best regards,

Krithika

entry.title: Re: SAP BO 4.2 SP05: Windows Server OS Upgrade From 2008...
entry.content: Sure, you would use Promotion Management to migrate the content from the old environment to the new one.

entry.title: Re: PowerDesigner "edit selection" button unresponsive in...
entry.content:
Hi,

sounds like a bug to me. Does it happen in the legacy report editor or in new-style editor? Did you try it on different PD installation (=different PC)? I tested it myself on PD 16.7 SP07 PL02 (new-style report editor) and it worked as expected (repeatedly Edit Selection was available and worked).

Regards,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: Re: PowerDesigner "edit selection" button unresponsive in...
entry.content: Maybe more detailed description (or better, reproduction steps) and screenshots would help.

entry.title: Re: implement a custom application that can scan QR codes...
entry.content:
Hi @priyankadegaonkar , 

I might be able to provide a bit of guidance, but note that I'm not a developer and this is not quite my area of expertise. 

Three years ago I released this blog on creating an App to scan barcodes via SAP Mobile Start. It might be outdated but in general concepts should stay the same and you could try to follow it.
Basically you need to deploy your app into the HTML5 repository of an SAP BTP subaccount that runs your SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition instance with your Cloud Fiori Launchpad. To this you will be able to connect SAP Mobile Start and use your app. 

With the SAP Mobile Start, the standard SAPUI5 barcode scanning component was not functioning on mobile devices outside the Fiori Client. So in the blog I used another js-library for the scanning part. With current UI5 versions though you should be able to leverage the ndc.barcodescanner component of SAPUI5. 
Based on the linked note it's build upon zxing-js library that should support your usecase of QR Code scanning.

Hope that's helpful!
Best regards, 
Dennis

entry.title: Re: Myth and truth about cluster & pool tables on HANA
entry.content: @jgleichmann-you do a really nice job explaining this, thanks.  Although your English is pretty much perfect there are a few stilted usages (my wife is an English prof so I get corrected all the time ;>]).  If before you publish your next blog you would like it proofread by a native (American) English speaker who knows ABAP then feel free to contact me, no charge of course.

3009061 - How to create tax code in S/4Hana Cloud

 

I hope this helps!

 

Best Regards,

SAP Product Support

entry.title: Article on Data Integration - MySQL
entry.content:
I have learnt many things from the course ‘Data Integration’.

Data quality is an important topic which deals with consistency and integrity of data. The quality of the data is to be maintained to be able to migrate from one system to another.

Data profiling is analyzing the data for possible risks or issues. This is very important as data must be clean and accurate to be fit for Extraction, Transformation & Loading.

Data cleansing deals with cleaning of data by applying various conditions and transformations. Mainly the wrong and corrupted data is cleansed in this process.

Batch processing is an important topic in Data Integration. It deals with collection of large amounts of data for the ETL process and data migration.

 Data Transformation is an important part of Data Integration. This portion deals with sourcing, storage and migration process.

Data Sourcing deals with the source data which must be extracted, transformed and migrated through various processes. Source data can be in any format like XML, XLS or DOC, etc.

Data storage refers to the storage of data in databases. There are many different databases where data can be stored. In SAP, we are having HANA Database. In frontend we are having MongoDB Database.

Data migration deals with sending of data from one system to another through various ETL tools after applying various transformation logics with conditions and set of rules, etc. There are various ETL tools which help in Data Migration like BODS, Hadoop, etc.

MySQL is a tool to read and interpret databases. I have learnt a lot of new topics from this course.

SELECT Statement is used to fetch the records from the database. The fields are stored inside the table which is in turn stored in the database. We are having the freedom to choose the fields which we need to fetch from the tables based on certain conditions.

WHERE is a condition in which we fetch the data from a table based on value of a field.

We also have some conditions like GROUP BY, ORDER BY. ORDER BY is used when we are trying to sort the data in ascending or descending order. GROUP BY is used along with aggregate functions like SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX, etc. Aggregate functions are used when we are trying to perform any arithmetic operation in SELECT query. (389 ) It is mandatory to use GROUP BY whenever we are trying to use any Aggregate function in the SELECT query.

JOIN condition is another important condition in MySQL. There are mainly four types of JOIN Conditions. INNER JOIN, LEFT OUTER JOIN, RIGHT OUTER JOIN, FULL OUTER JOIN.

INNER Join is condition between two tables where common matching records between the two tables will be fetched.

LEFT OUTER JOIN is a condition in which all the records from left table are fetched and only the matching records from right table are fetched.

RIGHT OUTER JOIN is a condition in which all the records from the right table are fetched and only the matching records from left table are fetched. (507).

FULL OUTER JOIN is a condition where it will return all the records when there is any match condition between both the tables.

I have definitely learned a lot and applied my learning in SAP ABAP Programming. It has led to sharpening my skills in MySQL and SAP ABAP.

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2024.001 Versão 1.10: Atualizações recentes na Nota Fiscal Eletrônica (NF-e)

entry.title: Re: Which CDS view is the corresponding MD04?
entry.content: how can we get C_DmndDrivenSupDmndList activated in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

entry.title: Re: Extending Fiori Elements, make a field read-only
entry.content:
It appears that this is not possible. By making this input field bound to a local JSON model, the SAP developers have made it impossible to override the field value or set its properties (read-only). 

This is a little hard for our client to accept; with all the promise of extensibility: Adaptation Projects, controller extensions, component configuration, key-user UI Adaptations, Personalizations, none of these layers provide a simple option to set a value of an input field. And it's such an important field, Billing Document Date has so many down-stream consequences in FI/CO.

Our client simply won't accept that there is no work around for this issue.

entry.title: Integrating SAP IBP data with Azure data lake
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are currently working on integrating SAP IBP with our Azure data platform to retrieve specific planning area and version data for our analytical dashboards. Could you please advise on which HANA tables would provide these details? Are there any standard tables available that store this information?

Thank you for your guidance.

Thank you

Prasad

entry.title: Re: How can we disable a particular search restriction?
entry.content: 1. Find all restrictions by Type

entry.title: Re: How can we disable a particular search restriction?
entry.content:
1. Or if there is case of specific search restriction, find that search restriction by Flexible query.
2. Before code execution, change the active flag and save it.
3. Post code execution enable the flag of search restriction

entry.title: Re: Cloud Connector, explained in simple terms
entry.content:
Hello dears,

COuld you please help me in answering these question? 

  1. You need to integrate the headquarters of a company running on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition with a subsidiary running on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.Which integration technologies do you use? Note: There are 2 correct answers to this question.
  • A. SAP Cloud Connector
  • B. SAP Cloud Integration Automation Service
  • C. SAP Integration Suite
  • D. SAP Open Connectors

Thank you!!

entry.title: BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CHANGE does not change delivery quantity since S4 upgrade
entry.content:
Hello,

we user this BAPI for several years (15) to change delivery quantity when delivery is just in process. After S4 upgrade now we get all the time return message VL 644 ("There is an unconfirmed transfer order"), what is right, but before we can change delivery quantity anyhow. It is important for us while loading over a longer period.

What has changed and what to do to work with it like before?

Thanks

Torsten

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
@David_Chaviano hello david
 
I’m Moaaz, and I’m thrilled to join the SAP Community Beginner Corner! I work with Edraky, where we specialize in delivering SAP training and implementation services to businesses throughout Africa and the Middle East. Our mission is to assist companies in effectively adopting and leveraging SAP solutions. Having joined the Beginner Corner, I’m excited to engage with the community, learn from seasoned SAP professionals, and contribute as I start my journey in the SAP world. Feel free to send me a private message now that I'm part of the group. I’m looking forward to getting started

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Process Automation Trail Service Error
entry.content: In the meantime, could you please try refreshing your page using Ctrl+Shift+R and let us know if it resolves the issue?

entry.title: Different Aggregation Modes for Non-Time and Time Root Attributes
entry.content:
Hello All,

I need two separate aggregations for the same Key Figure. If the KF is getting aggregated at time attribute level (e.g. day to month), aggregation should be MAX. If the KF is getting aggregated at non-time attribute level (e.g. Product to Product Group), aggregation should be SUM.

Similarly for disaggregation, if Disaggregation happens at time attribute level, it should be Copy Value and if it is non-time attribute, it should be Equal. 

How can I achieve this?

entry.title: 23. SAP Stammtisch St.Gallen
entry.content:
Bereits zum 23. Mal treffen sich SAP interessierte Menschen von Kunden, Implementierungspartnern und Freelancer zum lokeren Austausch im Rahmen dessen auch über SAP gesprochen werden darf.

Daneben gibt es immer wieder Themen aus Politik, Wirschaft und Kulturellem.

Melde Dich doch über den entsprechenden Link hier an.

entry.title: Re: Not able to log in to manager via SSO
entry.content:
Contact your Access Admin people and see what has changed in the IAS/IDP. Something has.

Certainly not related to licenses.

As a double-check, log on with a local ID (your super admin ID if you don't have any others) and make sure SSO is still enabled. If you can log on OK with that, then it is definitely something within your company and not SAP Enable Now itself.

entry.title: Re: Exclude Open Items from Interest calculation
entry.content:
Hello, I do not see a way to control this at the item level. You can exclude/include item(s) at the account level though. If the same account (debtor) has multiple items and they are in different documents then you can exclude/include these based on the selection parameters. But if they belong to the same document, then it is not possible to include few and exclude the rest. 

Please refer to this link for comprehensive information on this topic: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/62b0d6520c794a4ee10000000a423f68.html?advPhr=interest%20calculation&version=2408.500&locale=en-US

did you solve that issue? We have the same problem and are not able to copy the standard hierarchy to an inactive version...

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
For those who do not have data in the status text table, try running class /dmo/cl_flight_data_generator.  This should load that table with initial data.

 

Cheers,

Rich Heilman

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:

  • Data preview of the CDS View Entity

  • Logic in the ABAP class.

entry.title: Re: Are the fields Refkey 1, 2 and 3 available to use in...
entry.content:
Hello @R_Pooja R_Pooj

I am not sure which API you are referring to and I guess you are trying to post direct FI entry.

However there are few country specific fields are available as mentioned below which can be seen via below schema (Header Data) link of an API. Let me know if that can serve your purpose.
API Schema - https://api.sap.com/api/API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/schema 

- JrnlEntryCntrySpecificRef1
- JrnlEntryCntrySpecificRef2
- JrnlEntryCntrySpecificRef3

You can also refer below link.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/bb9f1469daf04bd894ab2167f8132a1a/13c22c58f5809344e10000000a4450e5.html 

Otherwise you should opt for extending an standard API as mentioned in the below link.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/extending-a-standard-api-a-guide-to-enhancing-api-qualitynotification/ba-p/13742428#:~:text=To%20begin%2C%20create%20a%20new,data%20source%20is%20a%20viewEntity.%5D

Best Regards,
Vijayendra

entry.title: Can you disable/change "Line Item Settlement" field on an asset master?
entry.content:
Hi expert

Can you disable/change "Line Item Settlement" field (XOPVW) on an asset master that was created when the class config was incorrectly set to AUC "Line Item Settlement"?

My client has created a large number of assets, while the asset class was incorrectly configured as "AuC with line item settlement".

Is there a way to remove the "X" in XOPVW (Line Item Settlement)?

Thanks in advance

The menu option can be choosen (see screenshot), but nothing happends. No popup, no selection. Other menu options do work.

entry.title: Re: Tree Table Hierarchy in Fiori Element based CAPM Project
entry.content: I want to display Tree Table Hierarchy in Smart Table based on CAPM project not want to use Standard Fiori

entry.title: Re: Onboarding 1.0 Not Prompting for ID Information
entry.content: UPDATE: The patch did NOT fix either of these issues. I am continuing to experience BOTH issues. I opened a high priority ticket as this is so ridiculous. SAP Product Management, Engineering, someone at SAP, needs to fix these issues already. It has been several months and these are compliance issues. I am not a happy customer.

entry.title: Re: CAP Application getting 404 after deployment to CF
entry.content: When you run CAP application locally, the server serves a generic index page with the list of services and their paths. This index page is not served in production. When the application is deployed to CF, it is considered in production. Even if the index page is not served, the services are served at the same paths as in development. You will have to replace the hostname in the URL for these services with the URL of the deployed app and access the services.

entry.title: Re: Step Into Autonomy: Introduction to Configuration Manager
entry.content:
Hi @camilapetry!

Very informative content, and a good way to reduce the efforts on support tickets 😉

entry.title: Limit the number of goals from populating in the performance form
entry.content: How to limit number of goals to populate in the performance form? Suppose if we have 10 goals in the goal plan and we want only 6 to populate in the goal plan? How to do this?

entry.title: ASUG Best Practices Conference
entry.content:
Greetings, 

We are excited about this year’s upcoming  ASUG Best Practices.  This year is different from before and that difference brings tremendous excitement and expectation.  We are pleased that this year’s event will bring together in a single location at the same time, not only your peers within the Oil, Gas, and Energy industry, but also the following industries:  Mill, Mining, Chemicals, and Industrial Manufacturing.  The event will take place on October 14-16, 2024 in Houston, Texas.

It will open with an insightful Vision Session that will include amazing digital transformation stories shared by companies, such as ExxonMobil.  Additionally, this year’s event will engage participants in the following experiences:

  • Keynotes with prominent SAP Leaders
  • 3 days of learning sessions, including SAP licensee case studies
  • Interactive formats like Deep Dives, Roundtables, Hands–on Demos, SAP Roadmaps, and more
  • 1:1 appointments with SAP experts (Member–Only Benefit)
  • Networking Opportunities to meet peers and make new connections

This dynamic event is expected to bring together over 1,000 participants.  Upon descending at the venue of this event in Houston, Texas, leaders will unite to address the biggest industry challenges.  As a participant in the event, you will depart with tools and resources to help you make the most of your technology investments.

As the industry continues its shift to reduce its carbon footprint, it remains imperative for energy companies of the future to adopt intelligent enterprise technologies to respond to present and future challenges.  This year’s event will be graced with speakers who will share best practices for how SAP cloud, industry-specific solutions, and partner solution and services within the SAP ecosystem are enabling their company to transition into a sustainable intelligent enterprise.

You may register for this three-day event using the following link:  ASUG Best Practices.      

Thank you and we look forward to your participation in this year’s event! 

Robert (Bob) Davis II, Global Upstream Oil, Gas, & Energy Lead, SAP IBU Energy and Utilities, Head of Upstream Oil, Gas, & Energy

entry.title: Purchase BOM (bill of material) concept in standard Purchase order
entry.content:
There is a requirement to setup a process in MM procurement area. Wherein the company would buy the Finished product (FERT Material type) from the supplier. The finished product is made up of child components (FERT material type). Whenever the Finished product (main item) is entered on the PO overview screen, the child components should all be populated underneath the same. The pricing and inventory must be at the main item level.

 

For example:

If company buys a ‘Desktop’ (main item) from the supplier. The child components of the desktop are ‘Monitor’, ’CPU’, ‘Keyboard’ & ‘Mouse’.

                When the ‘Desktop’ material is entered on the PO, automatically the components ‘Monitor’, ’CPU’, ‘Keyboard’ & ‘Mouse’ should get populated underneath the ‘Desktop’ line in PO overview screen. The main item must have the price and child items with zero value as company paying at the main item level

 

Is the above requirement can be attained from standard configuration? Using purchase BOM concept etc...

If yes, do let us know the steps to configure the same?

 

Note:

This is not a subcontracting scenario

#purchase BOM

entry.title: Re: Count function in webi
entry.content:
Try the following as a variable and place the variable in the Count column of the table.

=Count([Position Number]) In ([Username])

entry.title: CAP - CDS REPL to connect to db {kind : hana}
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am facinated with CDS REPL and use it quite often while exploring the CAP Bookshop sample. The cds.connect.to('db') method works perfectly fine with sqlite db in bookshop sample. However, I am also working on another project where the db configuration uses hana i.e. "db": { "kind": "hana" }. Moreover, the configuration is uses profiles viz., development, hybrid, and production.

I request your suggestions about how to use cds.connect.to('db') with {kind : hana}? How to use profile in CDS REPL? I tried cds repl --profile hybrid, but that does not work. The REPL is started, but cds.test() results in error. No DB connection is established.

I would appreciate if you could share some ideas / links / suggestions.

@qmacro 

Thank you.

Sudarshan

entry.title: SuccessFactors content import fails when a quiz is attached
entry.content:
Hello,

When I try to upload Articulate 360 SCORM files with a quiz attached into SF, the content import fails.

I never face problems when uploading SCORM files when no quiz is attached.

This is unusual because the quiz-related uploads always succeed in the test enviroment, but just not the live enrionment. 

Is anybody familiar with/able to resolve this issue? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: HANA Public Cloud validation _Precondition field/ f...
entry.content:
"Thank you for your reply. We need to build the validation at a global level across more than 70 countries. If we use the existing precondition field (as you mentioned above), it will impact other countries.

In our on-premises S/4 HANA system, we are using a validation with the following

prerequisite condition: SYST-TCODE = 'FB05' AND SYST-BATCH <> 'X' AND SYST-BINPT <> 'X' AND BSEG-HKONT IN SET

Check BSEG-WRBRT=" "

Error message " xxxxxxx  Now same validation we want to create in HANA Public cloud.

How an achieve this or We need to do custom development  ?

Thank you 

JP

entry.title: Re: Creating Supplier Quotation for Partail items in RFQ
entry.content:
Hello @Sabarinathan_En Sabarinathan_En 

Kindly refer to the app documentation "The system doesn't accept supplier quotations with the net order price that is equal to 0."
https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F1991')/S32  

Firstly RFQ to Supplier should be 1-1 as each supplier sends the quotation which you enter, based on that buyer decides which quote is to be accepted/rejected.
And if material are multiple for each supplier either wait or create separate RFQs.

Best Regards,
Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: Issue while downloading the Classification from the S...
entry.content:
Hi,

You might have an issue with Destination S4H_ODATA_INTEGRATION.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: SAP Learning auf dem 2024 Forum für SAP GTS edition for SAP HANA
entry.content:
Gehören Sie zu den Ersten, die das neue Release SAP GTS edition for SAP HANA kennenlernen, welches die komplexen Vorgaben im internationalen Handel noch effizienter abbilden.

Erfahren Sie, wie Sie Ihre grenzüberschreitende Logistik durch die Automatisierung und Straffung der Handelsprozesse beschleunigen und Ihr globales Handelsmanagement mit einem einzigen Repository für sämtliche Compliance-Stammdaten und Inhalte zentralisieren. So behalten Sie Ihre Kosten besser im Griff, verringern das Risiko von Bußgeld- und Strafzahlungen und werden vom Zoll schneller abgefertigt.

  • On-Premise- oder Cloud-Bereitstellung
  • Umfassende Verwaltung von Importen und Exporten
  • Verwaltung von Freihandelsabkommen und Zollverfahren
  • Selbstständiges Ausfüllen der Zollerklärungen und elektronische Kommunikation mit Zollagenten

Die drei eintägigen Events zu SAP Global Trade Services, Tax und Compliance und IAM und Security bieten Ihnen eine Mischung aus Experten- und Partnervorträgen, Networking und Diskussionsrunden.

Und auch SAP Learning ist vor Ort!
Lernen Sie unsere zahlreichen Lernformate kennen und erleben Sie in kompakten Demos, wie Sie auf unserer SAP Learning Plattform schnell und unkompliziert Ihr individuelles Lernangebot finden. Vertiefen Sie Ihr Fachwissen zu aktuellen Themen und erweitern Sie Ihr Netzwerk mit unseren SAP-Experten vor Ort.

Hier mehr erfahren und gleich anmelden: https://www.espresso-tutorials.de/sap-gts-2024/

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content:
Mi Solucion.

Saludos

entry.title: Re: From Monday Blues to Data Delights: My Geeky Hobby :)
entry.content: @NileshJadav - Thanks Nilesh!!

entry.title: New Course on SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Join this free course to get an introduction to SAP Integration Suite. Discover the key capabilities, defined enterprise-wide integration strategy, and how you can leverage AI to modernize your business landscape. Join the discussion group and prepare yourself for August 28 when more content will be unlocked.

Explore here: Modernizing Integration with SAP Integration Suite

Best regards
Eva Hett
(SAP Learning Team)

entry.title: Event-Based Revenue Recognition Time-Based Method for Service Contracts
entry.content:
Co Author: @Narinder_Hartala 

In this blog, we will focus on the Event-Based Revenue Recognition for Service Contract with Revenue Recognition Time-Based Method (Method 10).

First, Let's look at the Integration Overview of Service Contract Management with Event-based Revenue Recognition - billing create from Service Contract

 

Posting Logic:

Let’s assume that the following parameters are defined for your service contract:

  • Service contract for 1 year (January 1 – December 31)

  • Net value: EUR 1,200.00

  • Billing yearly

  • Billing Amount: EUR 1,200.00 (Billing Date = Contract Start Date = January 1)

The following graphic shows you the process steps for this method and the corresponding postings:

Configuration:

  • CCS: Allows you to use time-based recognition of revenue based on the billing plan for the end-to-end process of the service contract (corresponds to revenue recognition method 10).

  • CCSA: Allows you to recognize revenue time-based according to the billing plan based on a calendar year of 365 days. Costs are considered as they occur (corresponds to revenue recognition method 10).

  • CCSC: Allows you to recognize revenue time-based according to the billing plan based on a calendar year of 360 days. Costs are considered as they occur (corresponds to revenue recognition method 10).

In this blog, we will use revenue recognition key CCS for the service contract.

Demo:

1. Create Service Contract

Contract period for 1 year start from August, 2024 - August 2025 with Yearly Settlement.

Contract created and released.

2. Create Billing

After billing was created, run the Service Plan / Actual Report. We will see the revenue posted for the whole amount of this 1 year contract.

3. Run Revenue Recognition - Service Document

After Revenue Recognition Job completed, run the Service - Plan / Actual Report again.

Now, we can see that the revenue posting from Event-based is shown.

4. Event-Based Revenue Recognition – Service Document. Now we can see that there is Revenue Adjustment posting and Deferred Revenue Posting. Therefore, the Recognized Margin for this period 8 is 1000 USD

5. Create Service Order and do the Time confirmation

Link the Service Order to the Service Contract

Note that the service order that created under service contract will not be relevant for billing (As the billing should only be done from service contract)

Then with Advance Execution, Maintenance Order was created automatically from Service Order

We can see the link between Service Contract and Maintenance Order in Service Order History

Now we perform the time confirmation to the PM Order

After we have some expenses posted from the PM Order, we can go and check the Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Document again. Now, we can see the cost from the PM Order reflexing in the service contract as well.

6. We will run revenue recognition - Service Document for period 9

7. Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Document after run the revenue recognition for period 9. Now we should have 1000 more in recognized revenue for period 9.

And then the posting will continue to period 10, 11 and so on until it is completed the yearly period. This would help in spreading the revenue cross the year instead of recognized once in one particular period and create spike of the Margin. This is the purpose of using the Event-Based Revenue Recognition Time-Based Method for Service Contracts.

For other related SAP Event-based Revenue Recognition Blog that I have wrote earlier, you can refer to following

Event-based Revenue Recognition for Service Documents:

  • Event-Based Revenue Recognition for Service (Advanced Execution) - Time & Expense-based invoice EBRR

Event-based Revenue Recognition Blog for Projects:

  • Posting of Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Sales (4GQ)

Event-based Revenue Recognition Blog for Sales Orders:

  • Event Based Revenue Recognition - Sell from Stock (1K2)
  • Event Based Revenue Recognition - Sell from Stock (1K2) with Recognition Key - SFSCCM

entry.title: SAP Cloud Integration Advanced Event Mesh Adapter: OAuth [4]: Authorization Code with XSUAA
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Integration (aka CPI) offers an “Advanced Event Mesh Adapter” which is well integrated with the “Advanced Event Mesh” broker.
This article shows how to set up a scenario where we send a message from iFlow via “Advanced Event Mesh Adapter” to SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) with OAuth Authentication.
Today we focus on the OAuth flow “Authorization Code”.
This tutorial is based on XSUAA.
You might be also interested in authorization code with IAS, client credentials with IAS and client credentials with XSUAA.

Technologies covered in this blog post:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform, Cloud Foundry environment (BTP)
  • SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)
  • SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM)
  • OAuth 2.0, XSUAA

Content

0. Introduction
1. BTP: Configurations for User and OAuth
2. AEM: Configurations for OAuth and User
3. CPI: Configurations for Security and iFlow
4. Run Scenario
5. Optional: Alternative Configurations
6. Optional: Configurations via REST Calls
7. Troubleshooting
Appendix: Script for REST calls

Prerequisites

  • To follow this tutorial, access to  SAP Advanced Event Mesh is required.
  • Access to a BTP subaccount with admin permissions 
  • I recommend bookmarking the Security Glossary
  • Optional: REST client or any scripting for executing REST requests

0. Introduction

This tutorial is a sibling to the previous one which was based on IAS. The only difference is the usage of XSUAA instead of IAS.
Thus, only the differences are explained here, for all other descriptions please refer to the previous post. 

We want to send a message from an iFlow to Advanced Event Mesh Broker.
We want to use the Advanced Event Mesh adapter.
Authentication should be configured with OAuth.
OAuth authorization should be provided by XSUAA.
The OAuth flow should be chosen as “Authorization Code”

Please head over to the previous blog post and go through the intro.

In the following hands-on tutorial, we’re going through the following steps:
- create a user and register an OAuth client at the Authorization Server XSUAA.
- Configure the AEM (Resource Server) to support OAuth.
- Configure CPI (the client app) to use the OAuth client with “Authorization Code” flow.

1. BTP: Configurations for OAuth and User

This blog post is meant for those users who don’t have access to an IAS tenant.
Don’t worry, we can do “authorization code” flow with XSUAA as well.

1.1. The user

In our subaccount we navigate to “Security -> Users”.
We can see the available users, eagerly waiting to be used for testing.
They’re either living either in the Default IDP of the Subaccount, or they can be shadow users of a connected trusted IDP.
Both are fine.

 Now let’s go into the details screen of our good friend Joe Cool:

We can see 2 useful infos:
E-Mail: joe@coolmail.com
ID: 0e9047e5-7daa-4f20

Both can be used for identifying the user.
When the OAuth flow is performed during runtime, a JWT token will be issued by XSUAA and it will be contain both infos.
The user id will be written into the “sub” claim, the E-Mail will be written into the “email” claim

1.2. Create OAuth Client

 When using XSUAA, the approach to create am OAuth client is to create an instance of "Authorization and Trust Management" service.
Afterwards we can use the credentials for configuring CPI and AEM.
In this tutorial, we’re using the BTP cockpit for creating the service instance and service key.
Command line users may have a look at the appendix 1.

1.2.1. Create Service instance

We go to our subaccount -> Service Marketplace -> Authorization and Trust Management -> Create
Then select the service plan as “application” and enter a name of our choice.
In my example: "xsuaaForAuco".

 

On the next page, we paste the following JSON snippet:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

{
  "xsappname": "xsuaaForAuco",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "oauth2-configuration": {
      "token-validity": 60,
      "refresh-token-validity": 31536000,
      "redirect-uris" : [ "https://subdomain.integrationsuite-it-abc.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/itspaces/odata/api/v1/OAuthTokenFromCode"]
  }    
}

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

We finish the wizard.

Explanation

xsappname
The unique name identifying the OAuth client.
Would be generated, if we omit.
It makes sense to define an own name that is human readable.

tenant-mode
This would have to be adapted in case of multi-tenant applications.

token-validity
The duration of validity of our JWT token in seconds.
We’re using the minimum, 60 seconds.
In CPI, anyways the refresh token is used.

refresh-token-validity
Typically, the lifetime of an access token is short and refresh token long.
Here we’re using the maximum value, to avoid necessity of frequent admin interaction in CPI security material.

redirect-uris
To understand this setting - see introduction for Authorization code flow.
The CPI-Security Artifact is the client web application that requests a JWT token from XSUAA (with end user login).
XSUAA will send the "authorization code" (a short guid-string) to the "redirect URL" specified in the request.
CPI provides an own redirect-endpoint to receive the code.
But before XSUAA sends the code to that endpoint, it checks the config, to see if the URL is allowed.
As such, it has to be white-listed in XSUAA.
That’s why we can enter a list of redirect-uris in the xs-security.json descriptor file.
Wildcards are possible.
We need to enter the URL of the specific CPI tenant.
In my example:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

https://subdomain.integrationsuite-it-xxx.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/itspaces/odata/api/v1/OAuthTokenFromCode

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

You may adapt this sample url to your CPI-tenant.
The safe way is to copy it from the CPI-Security-Artifact creation dialog.
It is described in below chapter.

The documentation of the parameters can be found in SAP Help Portal:
https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/application-security-descriptor-configuration-syntax
In case of issues with Security Artifact: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003361116

1.2.2. Create Service Key

After service creation we click “view instance”.
There, we press on “Create Service Key”.

 

 We can enter any name of our choice, e.g. "myKey".
Press Create.

1.2.3. View Credentials

After creating the service key, we can view the credentials, i.e. the content of the service key.
We can either use the “View Credentials” button on the service details, or the context button of the new service key.

The content of the service key is a JSON object which contains 3 relevant properties:

🔸clientid
🔸clientsecret
🔸url

 

We should take a note of them.
This is the OAuth client, with its name and password, and the URL from where it can fetch an access token.
We need this information to configure both AEM and CPI. 

The BaseURL of the Authorization Server looks somewhat like this:
https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com

From this we can derive the endpoint that we will require below:

authorization: https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize 
token: https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token 
introspection: https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/introspect 

Always good to know the metadata-endpoint:

 

 

 

 

 

https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/.well-known/openid-configuration

 

 

 

 

 

2. AEM: Configurations for OAuth and User

Now we head over to AEM for defining the configurations required for OAuth authentication.
Our iFlow will establish a connection to AEM and will have to authenticate.
Instead of authenticating with username and password, we want to use OAuth.
As a consequence, AEM will receive a JWT token.
Which means that AEM will have to somehow check if the token can be accepted.
Below configurations enables AEM to do this validation.

At AEM, these steps are required:

- Enable OAuth (only once)
- Create OAuth Profile (for each OAuth client)
- Set default profile (in case of multiple profiles)
- create username

2.1. Optional: Enable OAuth Authentication

We login to AEM, click on Cluster Manager, choose our broker and click on the Manage tab.
In the section Event Broker Service Settings we click on Authentication.
We enable OAuth Provider Authentication and Save.

This step needs to be executed only once.

2.2. Create OAuth Profile

In Cluster Manager, we choose our broker and click on "Open Broker Manager".
There, we click on Messaging -> Access Control on the left navigation pane.
Below "Client Authentication" tab, we go to the sub-tab "OAuth Profiles".
To create a new profile, we click "+OAuth Profile" button and enter
Name
in my example "aucoXsuaa".

After pressing "Create" we can start to configure this new profile.

Note:
One profile corresponds to one OAuth client.
In our case, the OAuth client is an “Application” in BTP but it could be as well an "Application" in IAS or Microsoft Azure AAD, etc

Enabled
yes

OAuth Client ID
Here we enter the clientid which we copied in chapter 1.2.3
In my example, I enter sb-xsuaaForAuco!t12000

Change OAuth Client Secret
We should not forget to click here, although it doesn’t seem to be a field. 
Here we enter the clientsecret which we copied in chapter 1.2.3
In my example, I enter Aj4Qtb...
Note:
The client secret is required for AEM to call the introspection endpoint of XSUAA.

OAuth Role
This is a confusing setting.
Anyways, in case of OAuth flow, we choose Resource Server.
It makes sense, because in the OAuth diagram we assigned the role of Resource Server to AEM.
With other words, this is the setting for the “client-credentials” scenario.

Issuer Identifier
Another confusing field.
We can enter any string of our choice, as long as we use it when sending a request to AEM.
This value is an ID which helps AEM to find the proper OAuth profile for an incoming JWT token.
With other words:
At the end of the day, we send a JWT token to AEM.
AEM contains multiple OAuth profiles.
So how does AEM know, which profile to use for validating the incoming token?
The answer is easy to guess:
It uses this field .
First of all, the validator reads the issuer which is carried by the JWT (the property name inside JWT token is iss).
And it searches for a profile with the same Issuer Identifier.
Alternatively, we can enter any unique string in this field.
In that case, we have to send this same string in the request, as a special property.
(Documentation can be found here)

In our tutorial, we use the token endpoint of XSUAA.
This is the value of the iss claim in the JWT token
We use the url property from chapter 1.2.3 and adapt
In my example:
"https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token"

Discovery Endpoint
It is not required in our scenario.

JWKS Endpoint
It is not required in our scenario

Introspection Endpoint
This is an endpoint which is offered by the Authorization Server (XSUAA), we prepared it in chapter 1.2.3
In my example:
"https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/introspect "
The introspect endpoint is called to validate the JWT token.
The token is sent to the endpoint and the response contains a property ("active") which indicates if the token is valid. 

Authorization Groups Claim Name
We leave this empty.

Username Claim Name
Here we enter: "email"
(see optional chapter for alternative configuration)

Parse Access Token
We set to disabled.
Otherwise it would fail, as the JWT token which is issued by XSUAA, has a different format than expected by AEM.

Validate Access Token Audience
The aud claim in a JWT token contains a list of receivers that are intended by this token.
The client itself is always in the aud.
As such, we can enable the setting and enter the clientid which we received in chapter 1.2.3

Validate Access Token Issuer
We can enable this validation and enter the exact issuer which is contained in the iss claim of the JWT token.
In my example:
"https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token"

Validate Access Token Scope 
We disable this validation, because we don't require a specific scope.

Validate Access Token Type
We can enable this validation and limit to "jwt".

Finally, we can press “Apply” in the right upper corner.

 

 

2.3. Configure Default Profile

After creating an OAuth profile, it is ready to be used.
We've seen in the AEM dashboard that the new profile is added to a list of multiple OAuth profiles.
Now there's another setting in the "Client Authentication" Tab which allows to define which of the existing profiles should be used as default.

Within "Broker Manager" we go to
Access Control -> Client Authentication -> Settings
and press "Edit"
We make sure that "OAuth Authentication" is enabled.
Now we select our OAuth Profile which we created above as Default Profile Name from the drop-down.

And as usual:
Finally, we press “Apply”.

2.4. Create Username

This is an important section.
AEM requires a user context when sending messages.
In case of “Authorization Code” flow, we’re dealing with a user-centric JWT token.
As such, the real user (although probably a technical user) has to be created in AEM.
Not just the client ID, as we did in client-credentials scenario (previous blog posts based on XSUAA and IAS)

Now the first interesting question is: who is the user?
The answer:
The user who will be used for doing the login, below in chapter 3.1.
In my example: the user “Joe Cool”

The second question, even more interesting: what username to create?
The short answer: the email
The long answer:
It depends.
1. In the OAuth Profile above, we defined which claim does carry the required “username”.
In our example, we’ve chosen to use the “email” claim.
Alternatively, we could have chosen the “sub” claim, or other (first name etc).
To be on the safe side, we have to fetch a JWT token and look into the value of the claims, then copy the value of the chosen field.
In case of email it is easy, as we can just copy the email from BTP users screen.
See optional chapters below for a way to read the JWT token and for an alternative configuration.
Finally:
In our example, we create a username with value “joe@coolmail.com”.
The exact value has to be copied from BTP-subaccount -> Security -> Users

We go to Broker Manager -> Messaging -> Access Control -> Client Usernames
Create new username "joe@coolmail.com".
We "Enable" the user and press "Apply".

 

 

 

Note:
If the username is wrong we get an error at runtime: "403 Client Username Is Shutdown".

2.5. Retrieve Connection details

To view the data that we need to enter in the next chapter, we go to
Cluster Manager -> Select Broker -> Connect
We open any language and choose smf-Protocol -> Get Started
We need the host and the VPN name.
In my example:
Host URI: "tcps://mr-connection-abc.messaging.solace.cloud:55443"
Message VPN: "demovpn"

Optional: View Port Config

One optional last check, to be on the safe side:
Cluster Manager -> Broker -> Manage -> Advanced Options -> Port Configuration -> Expand Public Endpoint -> click “Edit”
Expand “Solace Messaging”
Make sure that the “Secured SMF Host” is enabled and the port number matches the above: 55443

 

2.6. Optional: Create Queue

If not yet available, we create a queue to which we’ll send events from iFlow.
To do so, we go to
Broker Manager -> Messaging -> Queues
and create a queue with name “testqueue”.

Recap

At AEM, we’ve configured OAuth via these steps:

Broker Service
- optional: enable OAuth Authentication
Broker Manager
- create OAuth Profile
- optional: set default OAuth Profile
- create username 

3. CPI: Configurations for Security and iFlow

At this point, the AEM broker is ready to receive JWT tokens issued for the OAuth client created in chapter 1.
Now we head over to Cloud Integration dashboard where 2 tasks are waiting for us:
- create a Security Artifact for handling the JWT token
- create an iFlow with AEM adapter for sending messages

3.1. Create Security Artifact

We create a security artifact for securely storing the credentials which we received in chapter 1.2.3.
CPI provides a special artifact type for OAuth 2 which is able to automatically fetch a JWT token with “Authorization Code” flow.
We go to Monitor -> Security Material
and click on Create -> OAuth 2 Authorization Code
In the dialog, we enter the following values:

Name
Any name of our choice.
Note that this name will be used in the iFlow.
In my example, "aucoXsuaa".

Provider
Generic

Authorization URL
This is the endpoint provided by the Authorization Server, to be used for requesting an authorization code and doing the redirect.
We enter the url which we stored in chapter 1.2.3.
The full URL in my example:
https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize 

Token Service URL
This is the endpoint provided by the Authorization Server, to be used for requesting a JWT token.
We enter the value of the url property of chapter 1.4.
The full URL in my example:
https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token 

Redirect URL
This is the endpoint provided by CPI, which is used as redirect endpoint for the Authorization Code flow.
The implementation will use the received code for fetching a JWT token.
We get this information, because we have to configure XSUAA with it.
We've done it already in chapter 1.2.1.
Now we can verify if it was correct.

Client ID
Here we enter the value of the clientid property which we copied in chapter 1.2.2.
In my example, I enter sb-xsuaaForAuco!t...

Client Secret
Here we enter the clientsecret property which we copied in chapter 1.2.2.
In my example, I enter j4Qtb...

Send As
We choose “Body Parameter”.

User Name
This is optional, we can enter our user "JoeCool"

Scope
We can leave this field empty

 Note that the credentials (clientid/secret) have to be the same as we’ve configured in the AEM profile.

 

Finally, we press "Deploy".

Do Login for Authorization Code

One more step is required after deploy:
We have to perform the "Authorization Code" flow, as it requires interactive user login.

If BTP is open in same browser but different tab, make sure to log out.
Otherwise this (admin) user will be used for the CPI artifact.
But we don't want it, we want the user "JoeCool" to do the login.
After pressing on “Authorize” in CPI Security Material screen, the login dialog of XSUAA must be displayed.
Here we login with the user we created in chapter 1.1.
In my example: JoeCool
As a result, we see a success message.
With this, we’ve enabled CPI to fetch a JWT token for us.
To be more precise: "Authorization Code" flow is done once, afterwards, the "Refresh Token" is used to refresh the JWT token without interactive user login. 

3.2. Create iFlow

Now we can go ahead and create the iFlow.
In this tutorial the iFlow is as minimalistic as possible, as we only want to show the usage of the AEM adapter.

We create a simple iFlow which does really nothing but sending messages to the Event Broker.
We define a hard-coded dummy message text.
We just want to make sure that the OAuth authentication works fine.
Let's quickly go through the steps:

Start Event
The iFlow is triggered once after deploy, by a "Timer" start event.

Content Modifier
In the "Message Body" tab we enter any dummy message text of our choice.

AEM Adapter: Connection tab
"Host":
The value for "host" is copied from AEM and pasted as is, no need to cut or append anything.
We've copied that value in chapter 2.5
In my example: "tcps://mr-connection-abc.messaging.solace.cloud:55443"

"Message VPN":
Same as above, the value of chapter 2.5.
In my example: "demovpn"

"Username":
We can enter our “joe@coolmail.com” user.
In our case, the username is not required, as it is contained in the JWT token (in the sub claim) and AEM will read it from there.
The username will be printed e.g. in the AEM log (see Troubleshooting section)

"Authentication Type":
Select “OAuth2”.

"OAuth2 Credential Type":
Choose "OAuth2 Authorization Code".

"OAuth2 Client Credentials Credential Name":
Here we enter the name of the security artifact created in chapter 3.1.
In my example: "aucoXsuaa".

AEM Adapter: Processing tab
Here we configure our queue.

"Endpoint Type":
We select "Queue".

"Destination Name":
Here we enter the name of the desired target queue.
In my example: “testqueue”.

 

That’s it for iFlow creation.
BTW, saving is always a good idea.

4. Run Scenario

Now we can deploy the iFlow and check the result:

In CPI :
The log at “Monitor Message Processing” should show success message:

In AEM:

 

We go to “Broker Manager” and check the number of “Messages Queued” which should have increased.

Consume:
Another option would be to use the “Try-Me” tool in AEM to consume the messages sent from iFlow.
We go to Cluster Manager -> Select Service -> Connect
We expand "Solace Web Messaging" and copy the “Connection Details”.
Then we go to "Broker Manager" -> Try Me 
and enter the connection details and the queue name.
Immediately, we receive the messages and can check the dummy content which we specified in the “Content Modifier” step of the iFlow.

With that we can be happy.
We've confirmed that OAuth authentication can be realized with CPI and AEM via AEM adapter.

5. Optional: Alternative Configurations

Let's briefly mention alternative configurations:

5.1. Use sub instead of email claim

AEM OAuth profile:
Parameter "Username Claim Name” has value “sub”:

AEM Username:
In chapter 1.1. we saw the user ID value in the user details screen.
We copy it and create a "Client Username" like "0e9047e5-7daa".

iFlow:
In the AEM adapter configuration we change the Username to "0e9047e5-7daa".
Now we can run the iFlow again and it will be successful.

6. Optional: Configurations via REST Calls

Please refer to chapter 5 of the previous tutorial:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-integration-advanced-event-mesh-adapter-oauth-authentication-2/ba-p/13778156

7. Troubleshooting

If the authentication is not configured correctly, the iFlow would fail during deployment.
The error could be seen in Monitor -> Manage Integration Content
Error message like below, security related error messages are usually not helpful:
com.solacesystems.jcsmp.JCSMPErrorResponseException: 401: Unauthorized

Status 401 indicates that the access is not allowed, token validation failed.
This means that the OAuth configuration is not correct.
To fix it, we need to go through the tutorial again and check:
- ClientID and secret: are they the same in CPI-Security-Artifact and in AEM-OAuth-Profile?
- Is the correct Security Artifact configured in the CPI-AEM-adapter?
- Go through the OAuth profile and check each setting and URLs, deactivate the validations.

CPI Security Artifact
Do new login in CPI Security Artifact, to fetch new Refresh Token, is always good.

CPI Security Artifact
In XSUAA we configured that the Refresh Token should expire after short time, I order to experience this error.
Once the Refresh token period is over, the security artifact is not able to fetch a new JWT token anymore.
* Error:
The error during deploy:
[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][RuntimeError] : {"message":"CAMEL_CONTEXT_NOT_STARTED"}
The error in test-iFlow-groovy script:
SecureStoreException: Problem during retrieving the access token for the OAuth2 Authorization Code credential aemAdapterTest
Access token request via refresh_token grant type for OAuth2 Authorization Code credential 'aemAdapterTest' failed
Refresh token is invalid or expired
Note:
The Security Artifact in the Security Materials list shows a green status although the token expired.
* Solution:
press authorize and login with the user.

Username missing
* Error
JCSMPErrorResponseException: 403: Client Username Is Shutdow
* Solution create username
The value must be equal to the value of sub claim
Or whatever claim is configured in the OAuth profile

AEM Logs
View the logs in AEM.
See Troubleshooting blog post for detailed description.
The command for connect:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

ssh demovpn-view@mr-connection-<yourID>.messaging.solace.cloud

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The command to view all logs:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

show log event

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Log should show (in this example, the usernameClaim is configured as sub):
INFO: CLIENT: CLIENT_CLIENT_CONNECT: demovpn … - username JoeCool OriginalClientUsername(JoeCool) … APIuser… authScheme(OAuth)
INFO: CLIENT: CLIENT_CLIENT_OPEN_FLOW: demovpn … username JoeCool Pub flow session flow name ….

Introspect JWT token
Create a script in the iFlow that uses the security artifact to fetch a token, decodes it and then print the token  content to the console.
Note:
In case of failure, the script is not executed, because the iFlow fails already during deployment, while the connection to AEM is established.
Hence, if we have issues, we need to modify the iFlow such that the AEM adapter is not used at all.
To do so, we just add an "End" step and connect the script to it.
Then run the script and view the content of the token in the CPI logs.
See appendix 2 for iFlow and script code.

Sample Content of JWT token for Authorization Code with XSUAA

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

{
   "given_name":"Joe Cool",
   "family_name":"Cool",
   "user_id":"0e9047e5-7daa",
   "user_name":joe@coolmail.com,
   "email":joe@coolmail.com,
   "sub":"0e9047e5-7daa",
   "user_uuid":"66ac2029-d82c",
   "ext_attr":{...},
   "xs.user.attributes":{},
   "xs.system.attributes":{"xs.rolecollections":[...]},
   "scope":["openid"],
   "client_id":"sb-xsuaaForAuco!t12000",
   "cid":"sb-xsuaaForAuco!t12000",
   "azp":"sb-xsuaaForAuco!t12000",
   "grant_type":"authorization_code",
   "iss":https://subdomain.authentication.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token,
   "aud":["openid","sb-xsuaaForAuco!t12000"]
}

 

Summary

In this blog post we've learned how to use OAuth authentication with "Authorization Code", for sending messages from iFlow via AEM adapter.
These configuration steps are required at AEM:

  • AEM Broker Service: enable OAuth Authentication
  • AEM Broker Manager: create OAuth Profile
  • AEM Broker Manager: define default OAuth Profile
  • AEM Broker Manager: create username

It is important to know which name has to be created as username.
This name is taken from the value of the JWT-token-claim, that is configured in the OAuth Profile (AEM).
The default is the content of the sub claim, which corresponds to the user ID in BTP (Security -> Users).

And we've learned how to configure the AEM adapter for an iFlow receiver channel:
CPI Monitoring: Create security artifact for "Authorization Code"
CPI iFlow AEM adapter: use the security artifact for OAuth2 authentication.
CPI iFlow AEM adapter: Connection details are copied from "Connection" tab in AEM.

In this blog post we've used XSUAA as Authorization Server.
We've focused on the OAuth flow "Authorization Code".

Links

OAuth spec https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749
AEM docu https://help.pubsub.em.services.cloud.sap/Cloud/cloud-lp.htm

XSUAA reference for oauth2 config: Application Security Descriptor Configuration Syntax

Blog: AEM Adapter with client credentials with IAS
Blog: AEM Adapter with client credentials with XSUAA
Blog: AEM Adapter with authorization code and IAS
Blog: Troubleshooting AEM

Appendix 1: Create XSUAA Instance via Command Line

For your convenience, please find below the details for creating an instance of XSUAA service

xs-security.json

 

{
  "xsappname": "xsuaaForAuco",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "oauth2-configuration": {
      "token-validity": 60,
      "redirect-uris" : [
        "https://subdomain.integrationsuite-it-abc.cfapps.eu12.hana.ondemand.com/itspaces/odata/api/v1/OAuthTokenFromCode"
      ]
  }    
}

 

The command:

 

cf cs xsuaa application xsuaaForAuco -c xs-security.json

 

Create service key:

 

cf csk xsuaaForAuco sk

 

View service key content:

 

cf service-key xsuaaForAuco sk

 

Appendix 2: Debug JWT token

Below screenshot shows my modified iFlow:

 And this is the content of the Groovy script:

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import com.sap.it.api.securestore.SecureStoreService;
import com.sap.it.api.securestore.AccessTokenAndUser;
import com.sap.it.api.securestore.exception.SecureStoreException;
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory;


def Message processData(Message message) {
   def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);

   SecureStoreService secureStoreService = ITApiFactory
       .getService(SecureStoreService.class, null);
   AccessTokenAndUser accessTokenAndUser = secureStoreService
       .getAccesTokenForOauth2AuthorizationCodeCredential("aemAdapterTest"); 
    
   String token = accessTokenAndUser.getAccessToken();
   messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("JWT encoded", token, "text/plain");

   String[] parts = token.split("\\.") 
   byte[] tokenDecoded = Base64.getDecoder().decode(parts[1])
   messageLog.addAttachmentAsString(
        "JWT decoded", new String(tokenDecoded), "text/plain");
    
    return message;
}

entry.title: Problem to get the missing parts
entry.content:
I have a problem to get the missing parts, the parts are not assigned in SAP to the machine.

Production order number 103828914

entry.title: How to configure XSUAA in CAP + MDK?
entry.content:
Good morning community, thanks for the help. I have a CAP OData V4 service running which uses authentication with XSUAA. I want this OData service to be consumed by my mobile application created with MDK. Until now I have been testing everything without authentication and it worked fine, but now I want to add this layer of security and I am finding problems that cannot be solved. I want to share some screenshots of what I have been doing and if you can guide me in being able to configure everything, it would be of great help.

My CAP service is called "InfraestructuraService" and at the moment it only requires authentication.

In my "packages.json" file I have the required authorization with "xsuaa"

In my "xs-security.json" file I configure oauth2:

In my app router xs-app.json I have the authentication mode set to "route".

In my CloudFoundry space, on my instances I have an "audit-auth" call:

I copied my Services Key information so I could use it in my connectivity settings in BTP:

By clicking on "Check Connection" I receive:

By going into "SAP Mobile Services" and then going to my application, within "Mobile Connectivity" I defined the following:

In the "APIs" section I can see my created destination which is the one I used previously in my BTP connectivity:

When I access my mobile application from the "Mobile Svcs" application I am not having any problems. But when I access from the web application I am receiving an error:

And then I get:

Can someone help me? I feel like everything related to XSUAA is getting more complicated than I would like.

entry.title: Re: SAP Design Studio Custom Community components aren't working after BWQ refresh??
entry.content: Hoping someone has run across this issue after doing a production to QA copy of data.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content: @MohammedMubeen01 it is great to meet you. I work on the same team as @sveabecker as a community advocate. I live in the US outside of Philadelphia. We are gearing up with community members for SAP TechEd. If you haven't yet please join the group to network with others attending & preparing. Best of luck! SAP TechEd - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Issue while downloading the Classification from the S...
entry.content:
Hi Manoel,
But in that case I shouldnt be able to transfer my order, GI and GR. Even the connection on destination gives a 403 resply.

Is there any way I can understand the error. 

KR,
Sayuj

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
entry.content: TASK1 Solution

entry.title: Re: Not able to log in to manager via SSO
entry.content: I checked with our Admin people but they said nothing has changed regarding IAS/IDP...I couldnt log in with a local ID yet though... will keep on trying thanks for the tip Dirk

entry.title: SAP Build Code Storyboard
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a CAP application with a draft enabled odata v4 service. In the storyboard under application logic I'm not able to create an event handler for the draft entity. (only for the entity itself)

Anybody an idea ?

entry.title: Re: Adobe Forms external image is not rendered
entry.content: Well, that is quite important step 🙂 Glad you found the issue
